summary: The needle must be placed more superiorly because children typically have smaller lower mouths and bigger tongues; thus, requiring less depth penetration during injection when performing this procedure on them versus adults. This adjustment ensures accurate targeting while minimizing risk associated with incorrect placement or injury from overly deep injections into younger patients' oral cavities. \boxed{}
